  "text": "## LLVM Pass Manager Registration and Execution Chain\n\n### 1. PassManager Template (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h)\n\nThe core PassManager is defined in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h` as a template class `PassManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (around line 162). It is parameterized by the IR unit type (e.g., Module, Function, Loop, SCC). The key convenience typedefs are:\n- `using ModulePassManager = PassManager<Module>;` (line 238)\n- `using FunctionPassManager = PassManager<Function>;` (line 247)\n\nThe `addPass()` method (lines 193-214) wraps a concrete pass in a type-erased `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, ...>` and stores it in the `Passes` vector as a `unique_ptr<PassConceptT>`.\n\n### 2. PassManagerInternal.h — Type Erasure Layer\n\n`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h` defines the abstract base `PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (virtual `run()` method) and the concrete wrapper `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>`, which implements the type erasure pattern enabling heterogeneous pass storage.\n\n### 3. PassManagerImpl.h — Execution Implementation\n\n`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h` provides the implementation of `PassManager::run()` (line 28). It iterates over the stored passes in sequence, calling `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)` and using `AM.invalidate(IR, PassPA)` to manage analysis invalidation after each pass.\n\n### 4. PassRegistry (Legacy Registration)\n\n`llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h` declares the legacy `PassRegistry` class with the singleton `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()` and `registerPass()` method. The implementation is in `llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp` (line 48: `PassRegistry::registerPass`). The helper template `RegisterPass<T>` in `llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h` (line 89) is the idiomatic way to register a legacy IR pass — its constructor calls `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)`.\n\n### 5. New Pass Manager Registry (PassBuilder + PassRegistry.def)\n\nFor the new PassManager, passes are registered via the `PassBuilder` class (`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h`, `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp`):\n- `PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM)` iterates over `MODULE_ANALYSIS` entries from `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def` and calls `MAM.registerPass(...)` for each.\n- Similarly for `registerFunctionAnalyses`, `registerCGSCCAnalyses`, `registerLoopAnalyses`.\n- `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def` is the X-macro registry listing all named passes (MODULE_PASS, FUNCTION_PASS, etc.) and analyses (MODULE_ANALYSIS, FUNCTION_ANALYSIS, etc.).\n- `AnalysisManager::registerPass()` (declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`) stores the analysis factory.\n\n### 6. Standard Optimization Pipeline (O2/O3) — PassBuilderPipelines.cpp\n\nThe O2/O3 pipeline is assembled in `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp` through these functions declared in `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h`:\n\n1. **`PassBuilder::buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ...)`** (line 1716): Entry point corresponding to -O1/-O2/-O3. It calls:\n   a. `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(Level, Phase)` — canonicalization + inlining pipeline.\n   b. `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(Level, Phase)` — aggressive optimization for speed.\n\n2. **`PassBuilder::buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()`**: Adds passes like `ForceFunctionAttrsPass`, `InferFunctionAttrsPass`, PGO, `buildInlinerPipeline()`, and calls `buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline()` via a module-to-function adaptor.\n\n3. **`PassBuilder::buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline()`**: Adds core per-function optimizations: `SimplifyCFGPass`, `SROAPass`, `EarlyCSEPass`, `InstCombinePass`, loop simplification (`LoopPassManager` with LICM, IndVars, etc.), and more.\n\n4. **`PassBuilder::buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()`** (line 1478): Adds post-inlining module-level optimizations: `PartialInlinerPass`, `ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass`, `GlobalsAA`, vectorization (`LoopVectorizePass`, `SLPVectorizerPass`), and late cleanup passes.\n\nThe execution chain is: `PassBuilder` constructs and wires AnalysisManagers → passes are registered via `addPass()` into a `ModulePassManager` → `ModulePassManager::run()` executes them in sequence using the type-erased `PassConcept::run()` interface, invalidating analyses after each pass."

  "text": "## LLVM Pass Manager Registration and Execution Chain\n\n### 1. PassManager Template (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h)\n\nThe core PassManager is defined in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h` as the template class `PassManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` (line 162). It is parameterized by the IR unit type (Module, Function, Loop, SCC, MachineFunction). Convenience typedefs:\n- `using ModulePassManager = PassManager<Module>;` (line 238)\n- `using FunctionPassManager = PassManager<Function>;` (line 247)\n\nThe `addPass()` method (lines 193-214) wraps a concrete pass in a type-erased `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, ...>` object and appends it to the `Passes` vector as a `unique_ptr<PassConceptT>`.\n\n### 2. Type Erasure Layer (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h)\n\nDefines the abstract base `PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` with a virtual `run()` method, and the concrete wrapper `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>` that holds each real pass and dispatches calls to its `run()` method. This is the backbone of LLVM's type-erased pass storage.\n\n### 3. Execution Implementation (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h)\n\nProvides the template implementation of `PassManager::run()`. It iterates over the stored passes in sequence, calling `Pass->run(IR, AM, ExtraArgs...)`, using `PassInstrumentation` callbacks for before/after hooks, and calling `AM.invalidate(IR, PassPA)` to propagate analysis invalidation after each pass.\n\n### 4. Legacy PassRegistry\n\n- `llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h`: Declares the singleton `PassRegistry` class with `getPassRegistry()` and `registerPass(const PassInfo &)` methods.\n- `llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp`: Implements `PassRegistry::registerPass()`, storing pass info in a map keyed by type info and command-line argument string.\n- `llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h`: Defines the `RegisterPass<T>` helper template — its constructor calls `PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)` to self-register a pass at static initialization time.\n\n### 5. New Pass Manager Registry (PassBuilder + PassRegistry.def)\n\n- `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def`: X-macro file listing all named passes (MODULE_PASS, FUNCTION_PASS, CGSCC_PASS, LOOP_PASS) and analyses (MODULE_ANALYSIS, FUNCTION_ANALYSIS, etc.) for the new pass manager. This is the central registry mapping textual pass names to pass constructors.\n- `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h` / `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp`: The `PassBuilder` class provides `registerModuleAnalyses()`, `registerFunctionAnalyses()`, `registerCGSCCAnalyses()`, `registerLoopAnalyses()` — each iterates over the corresponding entries in `PassRegistry.def` and calls `AnalysisManager::registerPass([&]{ return CREATE_PASS; })` to store the analysis factory.\n- `AnalysisManager::registerPass()` (in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`) stores the analysis creation callback, keyed by the analysis type's static `ID()`.\n\n### 6. Standard O2/O3 Pipeline Execution Order (llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp)\n\nThe pipeline is assembled by `PassBuilder` in `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp` (declared in `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h`):\n\n1. **`buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ...)`** (line 1716): Top-level entry for -O1/-O2/-O3. Adds `Annotation2MetadataPass`, `ForceFunctionAttrsPass`, then calls:\n   - `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()` — canonicalization + inlining\n   - `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()` — aggressive speed optimizations\n\n2. **`buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()`**: Adds `InferFunctionAttrsPass`, PGO passes, inliner pipeline via `buildInlinerPipeline()`, and `buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline()` wrapped in a module-to-function adaptor.\n\n3. **`buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline()`** (line 592): Core per-function passes including `SimplifyCFGPass`, `SROAPass`, `EarlyCSEPass`, `InstCombinePass`, loop optimization pipeline (`LoopPassManager` with LICM, IndVarSimplify, LoopDeletion), `GVNPass`, `SCCPPass`, `BDCEPass`, and more.\n\n4. **`buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()`** (line 1478): Post-inlining module-level optimizations: `PartialInlinerPass`, `EliminateAvailableExternallyPass`, `ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass`, `RecomputeGlobalsAAPass`, `Float2IntPass`, `LoopVersioningLICMPass`, `LoopVectorizePass`, `SLPVectorizerPass`, `VectorCombinePass`, `InstCombinePass`, `AggressiveInstCombinePass`, `SimplifyCFGPass`, and `GlobalDCEPass`.\n\nThe full execution chain is: pass class registered via `RegisterPass<T>` (legacy) or listed in `PassRegistry.def` (new PM) → `PassBuilder` registers analyses via `AnalysisManager::registerPass()` → pipeline is assembled by calling `PassManager::addPass()` for each pass → `PassManager::run()` iterates and dispatches each `PassConcept::run()` in order, managing analysis invalidation throughout."

## LLVM Pass Manager Registration and Execution Chain

### Key Files Found

| Role | File |

### Execution Chain

1. **Registration**: Passes self-register via `RegisterPass<T>` (legacy) calling `PassRegistry::registerPass()`, or are listed in `PassRegistry.def` (new PM) and registered via `PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses()` → `AnalysisManager::registerPass()`.

3. **Execution**: `PassManager::run()` iterates the `Passes` vector, calling each `PassConcept::run(IR, AM)` in sequence, then invalidates stale analyses via `AM.invalidate(IR, PassPA)`.

{"content":"1477: ModulePassManager
1478: PassBuilder::buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level,
1479:                                              ThinOrFullLTOPhase LTOPhase) {
1480:   const bool LTOPreLink = isLTOPreLink(LTOPhase);
1481:   ModulePassManager MPM;
1482: 
1483:   // Run partial inlining pass to partially inline functions that have
1484:   // large bodies.
1485:   if (RunPartialInlining)
1486:     MPM.addPass(PartialInlinerPass());
1487: 
1488:   // Remove avail extern fns and globals definitions since we aren't compiling
1489:   // an object file for later LTO. For LTO we want to preserve these so they
1490:   // are eligible for inlining at link-time. Note if they are unreferenced they
1491:   // will be removed by GlobalDCE later, so this only impacts referenced
1492:   // available externally globals. Eventually they will be suppressed during
1493:   // codegen, but eliminating here enables more opportunity for GlobalDCE as it
1494:   // may make globals referenced by available external functions dead and saves
1495:   // running remaining passes on the eliminated functions. These should be
1496:   // preserved during prelinking for link-time inlining decisions.
1497:   if (!LTOPreLink)
1498:     MPM.addPass(EliminateAvailableExternallyPass());
1499: 
1500:   // Do RPO function attribute inference across the module to forward-propagate
1501:   // attributes where applicable.
1502:   // FIXME: Is this really an optimization rather than a canonicalization?
1503:   MPM.addPass(ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass());
1504: 
1505:   // Do a post inline PGO instrumentation and use pass. This is a context
1506:   // sensitive PGO pass. We don't want to do this in LTOPreLink phrase as
1507:   // cross-module inline has not been done yet. The context sensitive
1508:   // instrumentation is after all the inlines are done.
1509:   if (!LTOPreLink && PGOOpt) {
1510:     if (PGOOpt->CSAction == PGOOptions::CSIRInstr)
1511:       addPGOInstrPasses(MPM, Level, /*RunProfileGen=*/true,
1512:                         /*IsCS=*/true, PGOOpt->AtomicCounterUpdate,
1513:                         PGOOpt->CSProfileGenFile, PGOOpt->ProfileRemappingFile);
1514:     else if (PGOOpt->CSAction == PGOOptions::CSIRUse)
1515:       addPGOInstrPasses(MPM, Level, /*RunProfileGen=*/false,
1516:                         /*IsCS=*/true, PGOOpt->AtomicCounterUpdate,
1517:                         PGOOpt->ProfileFile, PGOOpt->ProfileRemappingFile);
1518:   }
1519: 
1520:   // Re-compute GlobalsAA here prior to function passes. This is particularly
1521:   // useful as the above will have inlined, DCE'ed, and function-attr
1522:   // propagated everything. We should at this point have a reasonably minimal
1523:   // and richly annotated call graph. By computing aliasing and mod/ref
1524:   // information for all local globals here, the late loop passes and notably
1525:   // the vectorizer will be able to use them to help recognize vectorizable
1526:   // memory operations.
1527:   if (EnableGlobalAnalyses)
1528:     MPM.addPass(RecomputeGlobalsAAPass());
1529: 
1530:   invokeOptimizerEarlyEPCallbacks(MPM, Level, LTOPhase);
1531: 
1532:   FunctionPassManager OptimizePM;
1533: 
1534:   // Only drop unnecessary assumes post-inline and post-link, as otherwise
1535:   // additional uses of the affected value may be introduced through inlining
1536:   // and CSE.
1537:   if (!isLTOPreLink(LTOPhase))
1538:     OptimizePM.addPass(DropUnnecessaryAssumesPass());
1539: 
1540:   // Scheduling LoopVersioningLICM when inlining is over, because after that
1541:   // we may see more accurate aliasing. Reason to run this late is that too
1542:   // early versioning may prevent further inlining due to increase of code
1543:   // size. Other optimizations which runs later might get benefit of no-alias
1544:   // assumption in clone loop.
1545:   if (UseLoopVersioningLICM) {
1546:     OptimizePM.addPass(
1547:         createFunctionToLoopPassAdaptor(LoopVersioningLICMPass()));
1548:     // LoopVersioningLICM pass might increase new LICM opportunities.
1549:     OptimizePM.addPass(createFunctionToLoopPassAdaptor(
1550:         LICMPass(PTO.LicmMssaOptCap, PTO.LicmMssaNoAccForPromotionCap,
1551:                  /*AllowSpeculation=*/true),
1552:         /*USeMemorySSA=*/true));
1553:   }
1554: 
1555:   OptimizePM.addPass(Float2IntPass());
1556:   OptimizePM.addPass(LowerConstantIntrinsicsPass());
1557: 
1558:   if (EnableMatrix) {
1559:     OptimizePM.addPass(LowerMatrixIntrinsicsPass());
1560:     OptimizePM.addPass(EarlyCSEPass());
1561: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp%3FL1477-1560"}

32: class PreservedAnalyses;
33: 
34: // Implementation details of the pass manager interfaces.
35: namespace detail {
36: 
37: /// Template for the abstract base class used to dispatch
38: /// polymorphically over pass objects.
39: template <typename IRUnitT, typename AnalysisManagerT, typename... ExtraArgTs>
40: struct PassConcept {
41:   // Boiler plate necessary for the container of derived classes.
42:   virtual ~PassConcept() = default;
43: 
44:   /// The polymorphic API which runs the pass over a given IR entity.
45:   ///
46:   /// Note that actual pass object can omit the analysis manager argument if
47:   /// desired. Also that the analysis manager may be null if there is no
48:   /// analysis manager in the pass pipeline.
49:   virtual PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT &IR, AnalysisManagerT &AM,
50:                                 ExtraArgTs... ExtraArgs) = 0;
51: 
52:   virtual void
53:   printPipeline(raw_ostream &OS,
54:                 function_ref<StringRef(StringRef)> MapClassName2PassName) = 0;
55:   /// Polymorphic method to access the name of a pass.
56:   virtual StringRef name() const = 0;
57: 
58:   /// Polymorphic method to let a pass optionally exempted from skipping by
59:   /// PassInstrumentation.
60:   /// To opt-in, pass should implement `static bool isRequired()`. It's no-op
61:   /// to have `isRequired` always return false since that is the default.
62:   virtual bool isRequired() const = 0;
63: };
64: 
65: /// A template wrapper used to implement the polymorphic API.
66: ///
67: /// Can be instantiated for any object which provides a \c run method accepting
68: /// an \c IRUnitT& and an \c AnalysisManager<IRUnit>&. It requires the pass to
69: /// be a copyable object.
70: template <typename IRUnitT, typename PassT, typename AnalysisManagerT,
71:           typename... ExtraArgTs>
72: struct PassModel : PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...> {
73:   explicit PassModel(PassT Pass) : Pass(std::move(Pass)) {}
74:   // We have to explicitly define all the special member functions because MSVC
75:   // refuses to generate them.
76:   PassModel(const PassModel &Arg) : Pass(Arg.Pass) {}
77:   PassModel(PassModel &&Arg) : Pass(std::move(Arg.Pass)) {}
78: 
79:   friend void swap(PassModel &LHS, PassModel &RHS) {
80:     using std::swap;

{"content":"113: /// construction.
114: class PassBuilder {
115:   TargetMachine *TM;
116:   PipelineTuningOptions PTO;
117:   std::optional<PGOOptions> PGOOpt;
118:   PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC;
119:   IntrusiveRefCntPtr<vfs::FileSystem> FS;
120: 
121: public:
122:   /// A struct to capture parsed pass pipeline names.
123:   ///
124:   /// A pipeline is defined as a series of names, each of which may in itself
125:   /// recursively contain a nested pipeline. A name is either the name of a pass
126:   /// (e.g. "instcombine") or the name of a pipeline type (e.g. "cgscc"). If the
127:   /// name is the name of a pass, the InnerPipeline is empty, since passes
128:   /// cannot contain inner pipelines. See parsePassPipeline() for a more
129:   /// detailed description of the textual pipeline format.
130:   struct PipelineElement {
131:     StringRef Name;
132:     std::vector<PipelineElement> InnerPipeline;
133:   };
134: 
135:   LLVM_ABI explicit PassBuilder(
136:       TargetMachine *TM = nullptr,
137:       PipelineTuningOptions PTO = PipelineTuningOptions(),
138:       std::optional<PGOOptions> PGOOpt = std::nullopt,
139:       PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC = nullptr,
140:       IntrusiveRefCntPtr<vfs::FileSystem> FS = vfs::getRealFileSystem());
141: 
142:   /// Cross register the analysis managers through their proxies.
143:   ///
144:   /// This is an interface that can be used to cross register each
145:   /// AnalysisManager with all the others analysis managers.
146:   LLVM_ABI void
147:   crossRegisterProxies(LoopAnalysisManager &LAM, FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM,
148:                        CGSCCAnalysisManager &CGAM, ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM,
149:                        MachineFunctionAnalysisManager *MFAM = nullptr);
150: 
151:   /// Registers all available module analysis passes.
152:   ///
153:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c
154:   /// ModuleAnalysisManager with all registered module analyses. Callers can
155:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also
156:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.
157:   LLVM_ABI void registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM);
158: 
159:   /// Registers all available CGSCC analysis passes.
160:   ///
161:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c CGSCCAnalysisManager
162:   /// with all registered CGSCC analyses. Callers can still manually register any
163:   /// additional analyses. Callers can also pre-register analyses and this will
164:   /// not override those.
165:   LLVM_ABI void registerCGSCCAnalyses(CGSCCAnalysisManager &CGAM);
166: 
167:   /// Registers all available function analysis passes.
168:   ///
169:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c
170:   /// FunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses. Callers can
171:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also
172:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.
173:   LLVM_ABI void registerFunctionAnalyses(FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM);
174: 
175:   /// Registers all available loop analysis passes.
176:   ///
177:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c LoopAnalysisManager
178:   /// with all registered loop analyses. Callers can still manually register any
179:   /// additional analyses.
180:   LLVM_ABI void registerLoopAnalyses(LoopAnalysisManager &LAM);
181: 
182:   /// Registers all available machine function analysis passes.
183:   ///
184:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \c
185:   /// MachineFunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses.
186:   /// Callers can still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can
187:   /// also pre-register analyses and this will not override those.
188:   LLVM_ABI void
189:   registerMachineFunctionAnalyses(MachineFunctionAnalysisManager &MFAM);
190: 
191:   /// Construct the core LLVM function canonicalization and simplification
192:   /// pipeline.
193:   ///
194:   /// This is a long pipeline and uses most of the per-function optimization
195:   /// passes in LLVM to canonicalize and simplify the IR. It is suitable to run
196:   /// repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy important
197:   /// information about the semantics of the IR.
198:   ///
199:   /// Note that \p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are
200:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends
201:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly
202:   /// build them.
203:   ///
204:   /// \p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.
205:   LLVM_ABI FunctionPassManager buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline(
206:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
207: 
208:   /// Construct the core LLVM module canonicalization and simplification
209:   /// pipeline.
210:   ///
211:   /// This pipeline focuses on canonicalizing and simplifying the entire module
212:   /// of IR. Much like the function simplification pipeline above, it is
213:   /// suitable to run repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy
214:   /// important information. It does, however, perform inlining and other
215:   /// heuristic based simplifications that are not strictly reversible.
216:   ///
217:   /// Note that \p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are
218:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends
219:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly
220:   /// build them.
221:   ///
222:   /// \p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.
223:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(
224:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
225: 
226:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining as well as
227:   /// the inlining-driven cleanups.
228:   LLVM_ABI ModuleInlinerWrapperPass
229:   buildInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
230: 
231:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining with
232:   /// module inliner pass.
233:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager
234:   buildModuleInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);
235: 
236:   /// Construct the core LLVM module optimization pipeline.
237:   ///
238:   /// This pipeline focuses on optimizing the execution speed of the IR. It
239:   /// uses cost modeling and thresholds to balance code growth against runtime
240:   /// improvements. It includes vectorization and other information destroying
241:   /// transformations. It also cannot generally be run repeatedly on a module
242:   /// without potentially seriously regressing either runtime performance of
243:   /// the code or serious code size growth.
244:   ///
245:   /// Note that \p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are
246:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends
